enabled.description: Enable AWS services (S3, SQS, etc.) payload extraction and parsing

AgentTypeIface.title: Agent Type Interface
AgentTypeIface.description: Specifies which interface should the agent pick the IP address from in order to report it in the AgentIP field on each flow. Accepted values are: external, local, or name:<interface name> (e.g. name:eth0).

enabled.description: Enable Anthropic payload extraction and parsing

AttributeFamilyConfig.description: AttributeFamilyConfig maps, for a given record, each attribute with its MatchDefinition

metric_span_names_limit.description: Works PER SERVICE and only relates to span_metrics. When the span_name cardinality surpasses this limit, the span_name will be reported as AGGREGATED. If the value <= 0, it is disabled.

rename_unresolved_hosts.description: Will replace HostName and PeerName attributes when they are empty or contain unresolved IP addresses to reduce cardinality. Set this value to the empty string to disable this feature.

Attributes.description: Attributes configures the decoration of some extra attributes that will be added to each span

AttributesConfig.description: AttributesConfig stores the user-provided section for filtering either Application or Network records by attribute values

enabled.description: Enable AWS Bedrock payload extraction and parsing

Buckets.description: Buckets defines the histograms bucket boundaries, and allows users to redefine them

oneOf.description: Enable all propagation methods, disable propagation, or use empty string for disabled
List of propagation methods to enable (headers/http for HTTP headers, tcp for TCP options), separated by commas

ContextPropagationMode.title: Context Propagation Mode
ContextPropagationMode.description: Configures distributed context propagation. Can be 'all' to enable all methods, 'disabled'/'' to disable, or a list of specific methods: 'headers' (or 'http') for HTTP headers, 'tcp' for TCP options.

oneOf.description: A CIDR range (e.g. "10.0.0.0/8"). The CIDR string is used as the attribute value.

name.description: A human-readable name for this CIDR. Used as the attribute value instead of the CIDR string.

Definitions.description: A list of CIDRs to be set as the "src.cidr" and "dst.cidr" attribute as a function of the source and destination IP addresses. Each entry can be a plain CIDR string or an object with "cidr" and "name" fields.

bpf_pid_filter_off.description: Debugging only option. Make sure the kernel side doesn't filter any PIDs, force user space filtering.

default_exclude_instrument.description: Defines the default exclusion patterns that prevent self-instrumentation of OBI as well as related observability tools. It must be set to an empty string or a different value if self-instrumentation is desired.

default_exclude_services.description: Defines the default exclusion patterns that prevent self-instrumentation of OBI as well as related observability tools. It must be set to an empty string or a different value if self-instrumentation is desired.  Use DefaultExcludeInstrument instead

default_otlp_grpc_port.description: Specifies the default OTLP gRPC port (4317) to fallback on when missing environment variables on service, for checking for grpc export requests, defaults to 4317

exclude_instrument.description: Works analogously to Instrument, but the applications matching this section won't be instrumented even if they match the Instrument selection.

exclude_otel_instrumented_services.description: Disables instrumentation of services which are already instrumented

exclude_otel_instrumented_services_span_.description: Disables generation of span metrics of services which are already instrumented

exclude_services.description: Works analogously to Services, but the applications matching this section won't be instrumented even if they match the Services selection.  Use ExcludeInstrument instead

excluded_linux_system_paths.description: Executable paths for which we don't run language detection and cannot be selected using the path or language selection criteria

instrument.description: Selects the services to instrument via Globs. If this section is set, both the Services and ExcludeServices section is ignored. If the user defined the OTEL_EBPF_INSTRUMENT_COMMAND or OTEL_EBPF_INSTRUMENT_PORTS variables, they will be automatically added to the instrument criteria, with the lowest preference.

min_process_age.description: Min process age to be considered for discovery.

poll_interval.description: Specifies, for the poll service watcher, the interval time between process inspections

services.description: Selection. If the user defined the OTEL_EBPF_EXECUTABLE_PATH or OTEL_EBPF_OPEN_PORT variables, they will be automatically added to the services definition criteria, with the lowest preference.  Use Instrument instead

skip_go_specific_tracers.description: This can be enabled to use generic HTTP tracers only, no Go-specifics will be used:

EBPFBufferSizes.description: Per-protocol maximum bytes to capture per request per direction, sent to userspace via large buffer events. Values must stay aligned with MaxCapturedPayloadBytes and the k_large_buf_max_*_captured_bytes constants in bpf/common/large_buffers.h.  Default: 0 (disabled).

batch_length.description: Allows specifying how many items (traces/metrics) will be batched at the initial stage before being forwarded to the next stage Must be at least 1

batch_timeout.description: Specifies the timeout to forward the data batch if it didn't reach the BatchLength size

bpf_debug.description: Enables logging of eBPF program events

bpf_fs_path.description: BPF path used to pin eBPF maps

buffer_sizes.description: Limit max data buffer size per protocol.

context_propagation.description: Enables distributed context propagation. Can be a combination of: headers, tcp (e.g., "headers,tcp" or "all")

disable_black_box_cp.description: Disables OBI black-box context propagation. Used for testing purposes only.

dns_request_timeout.description: DNS timeout after which we report failed event

force_bpf_map_reader.description: Forces the PerCPU HashMap operation of the Network Flows reader. The system will always try "batch", which is more efficient, but legacy systems like RHEL8-based will fallback to "legacy" (the slowest, more resource-consuming iterate&delete approach).

heuristic_sql_detect.description: Enables the heuristic based detection of SQL requests. This can be used to detect talking to databases other than the ones we recognize in OBI, like Postgres and MySQL

high_request_volume.description: Optimizes for getting requests information immediately when request response is seen

instrument_cuda.description: Enables GPU instrumentation for CUDA kernel launches and allocations

kafka_topic_uuid_cache_size.description: Kafka Topic UUID to Name cache size.

max_transaction_time.description: Maximum time allowed for two requests to be correlated as parent -> child Some programs (e.g. load generators) keep on generating requests from the same thread in perpetuity, which can generate very large traces. We want to mark the parent trace as invalid if this happens.

override_bpfloop_enabled.description: Skips checking the kernel version for bpf_loop functionality. Some modified kernels have this backported prior to version 5.17.

payload_extraction.description: Configure data extraction/parsing based on protocol

protocol_debug_print.description: Enables debug printing of the protocol data

track_request_headers.description: Enables the kprobes based HTTP request tracking to start tracking the request headers to process any 'Traceparent' fields.

traffic_control_backend.description: Select the TC attachment backend: accepted values are 'tc' (netlink), and 'tcx'

wakeup_len.description: Specifies how many messages need to be accumulated in the eBPF ringbuffer before sending a wakeup request. High values of WakeupLen could add a noticeable metric delay in services with low requests/second. Must be at least 0 TODO: see if there is a way to force eBPF to wakeup userspace on timeout

enabled.description: Enable Elasticsearch payload extraction and parsing
Enable HTTP header and payload enrichment

rules.description: Is an ordered list of include/exclude/obfuscate rules.

EnrichmentConfig.description: EnrichmentConfig configures HTTP header and payload extraction with policy-based rules.

ExportModes.description: List of signals to export. If undefined or null, all signals are exported. If an empty list, no signals are exported.

ExtraGroupAttributesMap.description: Map of attribute group names to arrays of attribute names. Only 'k8s_app_meta' is currently supported as a key.

Features.description: List of metric features to enable.

enabled.description: Enable Google AI Studio (Gemini) payload extraction and parsing

bedrock.description: AWS Bedrock payload extraction and parsing

gemini.description: Google AI Studio (Gemini) payload extraction and parsing

cache_expiry.description: It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_GEOIP_CACHE_TTL for backwards-compatibility

cache_len.description: It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_GEOIP_CACHE_LEN for backwards-compatibility

GeoIP.description: GeoIP is currently experimental. It is kept disabled by default and will be hidden from the documentation. This means that it does not impact in the overall OBI performance.

GlobAttr.description: Glob pattern to match against the attribute value

cmd_args.description: Allows to limit by matching command line arguments

containers_only.description: Restricts the discovery to processes which are running inside a container

exe_path.description: Allows defining the regular expression matching the full executable path.

exports.description: Configures what to export. Allowed values are 'metrics', 'traces', or an empty array (disabled). An unspecified value (nil) will use the default configuration value

k8s_pod_annotations.description: Allows matching against the annotations of a pod

k8s_pod_labels.description: Allows matching against the labels of a pod

languages.description: Language allows defining services to instrument based on the programming language they are written in. Use lowercase names, e.g. java,go

metrics.description: Configuration that is custom for this service match

name.description: Will define a name for the matching service. If unset, it will take the name of the executable process, from the OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var of the instrumented process, or from other metadata like Kubernetes annotations.  Name should be set in the instrumentation target via kube metadata or standard env vars.  To be kept undocumented until we remove it.

namespace.description: Will define a namespace for the matching service. If unset, it will be left empty.  Namespace should be set in the instrumentation target via kube metadata or standard env vars.  To be kept undocumented until we remove it.

open_ports.description: Allows defining a group of ports that this service could open. It accepts a comma-separated list of port numbers (e.g. 80) and port ranges (e.g. 8080-8089)

target_pids.description: Allows selecting processes by PID (static from config). When non-empty, the process PID must be in this list.

GlobDefinitionCriteria.description: GlobDefinitionCriteria allows defining a group of services to be instrumented according to a set of attributes. If a given executable/service matches multiple of the attributes, the earliest defined service will take precedence.

enabled.description: Enable GraphQL payload extraction and parsing

enrichment.description: Configures HTTP header and payload extraction with policy-based rules

sqlpp.description: SQL++ payload extraction and parsing (Couchbase and other SQL++ databases)

HTTPParsingDefaultAction.description: HTTPParsingDefaultAction specifies the default action per rule type.

case_sensitive.description: Controls whether header matching is case-sensitive (headers only)

methods.description: Is a list of HTTP methods this rule applies to (shared). Empty means all methods.

obfuscation_json_paths.description: Is a list of JSONPath expressions for fields to obfuscate (body only)

patterns.description: Is a list of glob patterns to match header names against (headers only)

url_path_patterns.description: Is a list of glob patterns to match the request path against (shared)

HTTPParsingMatch.description: HTTPParsingMatch defines matching criteria for an HTTP parsing rule. Header rules use Patterns and CaseSensitive. Body rules use ObfuscationJSONPaths. URLPathPatterns and Methods are shared across both types.

default_action.description: Specifies what to do when no rule matches, per type.

obfuscation_string.description: Is the replacement string used when a rule's action is "obfuscate"

HTTPParsingPolicy.description: HTTPParsingPolicy defines the default action for http enrichment rules.

match.description: Defines the matching criteria for this rule

type.description: Specifies what this rule matches against: "headers" or "body"

HTTPParsingRule.description: HTTPParsingRule defines a single include/exclude/obfuscate rule for HTTP header and payload extraction.

override.description: Allows overriding the reported host.id in OBI

path.description: It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_GEOIP_IPINFO_PATH for backwards-compatibility

exclude.description: Exclude will remove attributes from the include list (or the default attribute set). It can be an attribute name or a wildcard.

include.description: Include is a list of metric attributes that have to be reported. It can be an attribute name or a wildcard (e.g. k8s.dst.* to include all the attributes starting with k8s.dst). If no include list is provided, the default attribute set will be reported.

dns.description: Controls whether OBI uses the DNS to resolve the local hostname. If false, the local hostname is used as-is.

override_hostname.description: Can be optionally set to avoid resolving any hostname and using this value. OBI will anyway attach the process ID to the given hostname for composing the instance ID.

InstanceIDConfig.description: InstanceIDConfig configures how OBI will get the Instance ID of the traces/metrics from the current hostname + the instrumented process PID

IntEnum.description: IntEnum defines an enumeration of integers (e.g. ports or PIDs). It allows a set of single values or ranges. When unmarshalled from text, it accepts a comma-separated list (e.g. 80,443,8000-8999). When unmarshalled from YAML, it accepts either a scalar (same as text) or a sequence (e.g. [1234, 5678]).

IntRange.description: A comma-separated list of numerics or numeric ranges

InternalMetricsConfig.description: InternalMetricsConfig options for the different metrics exporters

enabled.description: Enable JSON-RPC payload extraction and parsing

cluster_name.description: Overrides cluster name. If empty, the NetO11y module will try to retrieve it from the Cloud Provider Metadata (EC2, GCP and Azure), and leave it empty if it fails to.

disable_informers.description: Allows selectively disabling some informers. Accepted value is a list that might contain node or service. Disabling any of them will cause metadata to be incomplete but will reduce the load of the Kube API. Pods informer can't be disabled. For that purpose, you should disable the whole kubernetes metadata decoration.

drop_external.description: Will drop, in NetO11y component, any flow where the source or destination IPs are not matched to any kubernetes entity, assuming they are cluster-external

informers_resync_period.description: Defaults to 30m. Higher values will reduce the load on the Kube API.

informers_sync_timeout.description: Specifies the timeout for waiting for informers to sync on startup.

kubeconfig_path.description: Specifies the path to the kubeconfig file. If unset, it will look in the usual location.

meta_cache_address.description: Specifies the host:port address of the obi-k8s-cache service instance

meta_restrict_local_node.description: Will download only the metadata from the Pods that are located in the same node as the OBI instance. It will also restrict the Node information to the local node.

meta_source_labels.description: Allows OBI overriding the service name and namespace of an application from the given labels.  kept for backwards-compatibility with Beyla 1.9

reconnect_initial_interval.description: Specifies the time to wait before reconnecting to the Kubernetes API after a connection loss.

resource_labels.description: Allows OBI overriding the OTEL Resource attributes from a map of user-defined labels.

service_name_template.description: Allows to override the service.name with a custom value. Uses the go template language.

async_writer_channel_len.description: Defines the capacity of every shard's channel for the async log writer Default: 500

async_writer_workers.description: Defines the number of shards for the async log writer Default: 8

cache_size.description: Defines the maximum number of cached file descriptors Default: 128

cache_ttl.description: Defines the TTL for cached file descriptors Default: 30m

services.description: Specifies the services to enable log enrichment for

service.description: Should also be contained in 'services' in the Discovery section

global_scale_factor.description: Scales map sizes in powers of two:   > 0: grows size (2x per step)   < 0: shrinks size (1/2 per step)   = 0: no change

greater_than.description: Numerical comparison for e.g. http.code or timings

match.description: Stores the glob that a given attribute must match to let the record pass

not_match.description: Stores the glob that a given attribute MUST NOT match to let the record pass

MatchDefinition.description: MatchDefinition stores the user-provided definition for the record filtering.

asn_path.description: It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_GEOIP_MAXMIND_ASN_PATH for backwards-compatibility

MetaSourceLabels.description: MetaSourceLabels allow overriding some metadata from kubernetes labels, Left for backwards-compatibility.

OTELIntervalMS.description: Supports metric intervals as specified by the standard OTEL definition. OTEL_EBPF_METRICS_INTERVAL takes precedence over it.

extra_span_resource_attributes.description: Adds extra metadata labels to OTEL span metrics from sources whose availability can't be known beforehand. For example, to add the OTEL deployment.environment resource attribute as a OTEL resource attribute, you should add `deployment.environment`.

features.description: Specifies which metric features to export. Accepted values: application, network, application_span, application_service_graph, ... envDefault is provided to avoid breaking changes  use top-level MetricsConfig.Features instead.

insecure_skip_verify.description: Enables skipping TLS certificate verification (not standard, so we don't follow the same naming convention)

instrumentations.description: Allows configuration of which instrumentations should be enabled, e.g. http, grpc, sql...

otel_sdk_log_level.description: Works independently from the global LogLevel because it prints GBs of logs in Debug mode and the Info messages leak internal details that are not usually valuable for the final user. Accepted values: debug, info, warn, error (case-insensitive).

ttl.description: Specifies the time since a metric was updated for the last time until it is removed from the metrics set.

cache_expiry.description: Specifies the time-to-live of a cached IP->hostname entry. After the cached entry becomes older than this time, the IP->hostname entry will be looked up again.

cache_len.description: Specifies the max size of the LRU cache that is checked before performing the name lookup. Default: 256

sources.description: Specifies the backends used for name resolving. Accepted values: dns, k8s, rdns

agent_ip.description: Allows overriding the reported Agent IP address on each flow.

agent_ip_iface.description: Specifies which interface should the agent pick the IP address from in order to report it in the AgentIP field on each flow. Accepted values are: external (default), local, or name:<interface name> (e.g. name:eth0). If the AgentIP configuration property is set, this property has no effect.

agent_ip_type.description: Specifies which type of IP address (IPv4 or IPv6 or any) should the agent report in the AgentID field of each flow. Accepted values are: any (default), ipv4, ipv6. If the AgentIP configuration property is set, this property has no effect.

cache_active_timeout.description: Specifies the maximum duration that flows are kept in the accounting cache before being flushed for its later export.

cache_max_flows.description: Specifies how many flows can be accumulated in the accounting cache before being flushed for its later export. Default value is 5000. Decrease it if you see the "received message larger than max" error in OBI logs.

cidrs.description: List, to be set as the "src.cidr" and "dst.cidr" attribute as a function of the source and destination IP addresses. If an IP does not match any address here, the attributes won't be set. If an IP matches multiple CIDR definitions, the flow will be decorated with the narrowest CIDR. By this reason, you can safely add a 0.0.0.0/0 entry to group there all the traffic that does not match any of the other CIDRs.

deduper.description: Specifies the deduper type. Accepted values are "none" (disabled) and "first_come". When enabled, it will detect duplicate flows (flows that have been detected e.g. through both the physical and a virtual interface). "first_come" will forward only flows from the first interface the flows are received from. Default value: first_come

deduper_fc_ttl.description: Specifies the expiry duration of the flows "first_come" deduplicator. After a flow hasn't been received for that expiry time, the deduplicator forgets it. That means that a flow from a connection that has been inactive during that period could be forwarded again from a different interface. If the value is not set, it will default to 2 * CacheActiveTimeout

direction.description: Allows selecting which flows to trace according to its direction. Accepted values are "ingress", "egress" or "both" (default).

enable.description: Network metrics. Default value is false (disabled)  add "network" or "network_inter_zone" to OTEL_EBPF_METRICS_FEATURES  TODO OBI 3.0: remove

exclude_interfaces.description: Contains the interface names that will be excluded from flow tracing. Default: "lo" (loopback). If an entry is enclosed by slashes (e.g. `/br-/`), it will match as regular expression, otherwise it will be matched as a case-sensitive string.

exclude_protocols.description: Causes OBI to drop flows whose transport protocol is in this list. If the Protocols list is already defined, ExcludeProtocols has no effect.

guess_ports.description: Controls how OBI assigns server.port/client.port when the connection initiator is unknown. Accepted values are "ordinal" (assume highest port is client) and "disable" (default, do not guess and emit empty client/server port attributes for unknown-initiator flows).

interfaces.description: Contains the interface names from where flows will be collected. If empty, the agent will fetch all the interfaces in the system, excepting the ones listed in ExcludeInterfaces. If an entry is enclosed by slashes (e.g. `/br-/`), it will match as regular expression, otherwise it will be matched as a case-sensitive string.

listen_interfaces.description: Specifies the mechanism used by the agent to listen for added or removed network interfaces. Accepted values are "watch" (default) or "poll". If the value is "watch", interfaces are traced immediately after they are created. This is the recommended setting for most configurations. "poll" value is a fallback mechanism that periodically queries the current network interfaces (frequency specified by ListenPollPeriod).

listen_poll_period.description: Specifies the periodicity to query the network interfaces when the ListenInterfaces value is set to "poll".

print_flows.description: Enables printing the network flows to the Standard Output

protocols.description: Causes OBI to drop flows whose transport protocol is not in this list.

reverse_dns.description: Allows flows that haven't been previously decorated with any source/destination name to override the name with the network hostname of the source and destination IPs. This is an experimental feature and it is not guaranteed to work on most virtualized environments for external traffic.

sampling.description: Holds the rate at which packets should be sampled and sent to the target collector. E.g. if set to 100, one out of 100 packets, on average, will be sent to the target collector.

source.description: Specify the source type for network events, e.g tc or socket_filter. The tc implementation cannot be used when there are other tc eBPF probes, e.g. Cilium CNI.

enabled.description: Enable OpenAI payload extraction and parsing

exemplar_filter.description: Controls when exemplars are attached to metrics. Accepted values: "always_on", "always_off", "trace_based". Defaults to "always_off": do not attach exemplars. This mimics the OTEL_METRICS_EXEMPLAR_FILTER specification.

extra_resource_attributes.description: Adds extra metadata labels to Prometheus metrics from sources whose availability can't be known beforehand. For example, to add the OTEL deployment.environment resource attribute as a Prometheus resource attribute, you should add `deployment.environment`.

extra_span_resource_attributes.description: Adds extra metadata labels to Prometheus span metrics from sources whose availability can't be known beforehand. For example, to add the OTEL deployment.environment resource attribute as a Prometheus resource attribute, you should add `deployment.environment`.

features.description: Features specifies which metric features to export. Accepted values: application, network, application_span, application_service_graph, ...  use top-level MetricsConfig.Features instead.

instrumentations.description: Allows configuration of which instrumentations should be enabled, e.g. http, grpc, sql...

ttl.description: Specifies the time since a metric was updated for the last time until it is removed from the metrics set.

RegexDefinitionCriteria.description: RegexDefinitionCriteria allows defining a group of services to be instrumented according to a set of attributes. If a given executable/service matches multiple of the attributes, the earliest defined service will take precedence.

cmd_args.description: Allows matching by command line arguments

containers_only.description: Restrict the discovery to processes which are running inside a container

exe_path.description: Allows defining the regular expression matching the full executable path.

exports.description: Configures what to export. Allowed values are 'metrics', 'traces', or an empty array (disabled). An unspecified value (nil) will use the default configuration value

k8s_pod_annotations.description: Allows matching against the annotations of a pod

k8s_pod_labels.description: Allows matching against the labels of a pod

languages.description: Language allows defining services to instrument based on the programming language they are written in.

metrics.description: Configuration that is custom for this service match

name.description: Will define a name for the matching service. If unset, it will take the name of the executable process, from the OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var of the instrumented process, or from other metadata like Kubernetes annotations.  Name should be set in the instrumentation target via kube metadata or standard env vars.  To be kept undocumented until we remove it.

namespace.description: Will define a namespace for the matching service. If unset, it will be left empty.  Namespace should be set in the instrumentation target via kube metadata or standard env vars.  To be kept undocumented until we remove it.

open_ports.description: Allows defining a group of ports that this service could open. It accepts a comma-separated list of port numbers (e.g. 80) and port ranges (e.g. 8080-8089)

target_pids.description: Allows selecting processes by PID. When non-empty, the process PID must be in this list (in addition to any path/port criteria).

RegexSelector.description: RegexSelector that specify a given instrumented service. Each instance has to define either the OpenPorts or Path property, or both. These are used to match a given executable. If both OpenPorts and Path are defined, the inspected executable must fulfill both properties.

RegexpAttr.description: Regular expression to match against the executable file path

max_interval.description: Specifies the upper bound on the wait duration between consecutive retry attempts.

start_interval.description: Specifies the initial wait duration between the first and second retry attempt.

timeout.description: Specifies the maximum total time allowed for all retry attempts before giving up.

RetryConfig.description: RetryConfig holds the retry policy for metadata fetch operations. It controls the exponential backoff used in physical node, cloud instance or local virtual machine.

cache_expiry.description: Only applies to the "local" and "ebpf" ReverseDNS type. It specifies the time-to-live of a cached IP->hostname entry. After the cached entry becomes older than this time, the IP->hostname entry will be looked up again. It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_REVERSE_DNS_CACHE_TTL for backwards-compatibility

cache_len.description: Only applies to the "local" and "ebpf" ReverseDNS type. It specifies the max size of the LRU cache that is checked before performing the name lookup. Default: 256 It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_REVERSE_DNS_CACHE_LEN for backwards-compatibility

type.description: Specifies the ReverseDNS method. Values are "none" (default), "local" and "ebpf" It also accepts OTEL_EBPF_NETWORK_REVERSE_DNS_TYPE for backwards-compatibility

ReverseDNS.description: ReverseDNS is currently experimental. It is kept disabled by default and will be hidden from the documentation. This means that it does not impact in the overall OBI performance.

ignore_mode.description: To be removed and replaced by a collector-like filtering mechanism

ignored_patterns.description: To be removed and replaced by a collector-like filtering mechanism

max_path_segment_cardinality.description: Max allowed path segment cardinality (per service) for the heuristic matcher

patterns.description: Defines the URL path patterns that will match to a route

unmatched.description: Specifies what to do when a route pattern is not matched

wildcard_char.description: Character that will be used to replace route segments

RoutesConfig.description: RoutesConfig allows grouping URLs sharing a given pattern.

enabled.description: Enable SQL++ payload extraction and parsing

endpoint_patterns.description: Specifies URL path patterns to detect SQL++ endpoints Example: ["/query/service", "/query"]

SamplerConfig.description: Sampler standard configuration https://opentelemetry.io/docs/concepts/sdk-configuration/general-sdk-configuration/#otel_traces_sampler We don't support, yet, the jaeger and xray samplers.

Selection.description: Selection specifies which attributes are allowed for each metric. The key is the metric name (either in Prometheus or OpenTelemetry format) The value is the enumeration of included/excluded attribute globs

agent_ip.description: Allows overriding the reported Agent IP address on each stat.

agent_ip_iface.description: Specifies which interface should the agent pick the IP address from in order to report it in the AgentIP field on each stat. Accepted values are: external (default), local, or name:<interface name> (e.g. name:eth0). If the AgentIP configuration property is set, this property has no effect.

agent_ip_type.description: Specifies which type of IP address (IPv4 or IPv6 or any) should the agent report in the AgentID field of each stat. Accepted values are: any (default), ipv4, ipv6. If the AgentIP configuration property is set, this property has no effect.

cidrs.description: List, to be set as the "src.cidr" and "dst.cidr" attribute as a function of the source and destination IP addresses. If an IP does not match any address here, the attributes won't be set. If an IP matches multiple CIDR definitions, the stat will be decorated with the narrowest CIDR. By this reason, you can safely add a 0.0.0.0/0 entry to group there all the traffic that does not match any of the other CIDRs.

print_stats.description: Enables printing the stats to the Standard Output

reverse_dns.description: Allows stats that haven't been previously decorated with any source/destination name to override the name with the network hostname of the source and destination IPs. This is an experimental feature and it is not guaranteed to work on most virtualized environments for external traffic.

StatsConfig.description: TODO: see if there is a way to merge common fields with NetworkConfig

features.description: Specifies which metric features to export. Accepted values: application, network, application_span, application_service_graph, ... envDefault is provided to avoid breaking changes

SvcMetricsConfig.description: SvcMetricsConfig is equivalent to MetricsConfig, but avoids defining environment variable, since this is a per-service configuration that needs to be defined exclusively in the service definition YAML.

backoff_initial_interval.description: Configuration options for BackOffConfig of the traces exporter. See https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/config/configretry/backoff.go BackOffInitialInterval the time to wait after the first failure before retrying.

backoff_max_elapsed_time.description: Specifies the maximum amount of time (including retries) spent trying to send a request/batch.

backoff_max_interval.description: Specifies the upper bound on backoff interval.

batch_max_size.description: Is the maximum number of spans that the batcher will accumulate before flushing a batch to the sending queue.

batch_timeout.description: Is the time after which a batch will be sent regardless of its size.

insecure_skip_verify.description: Enables skipping TLS certificate verification (not standard, so we don't follow the same naming convention)

instrumentations.description: Allows configuration of which instrumentations should be enabled, e.g. http, grpc, sql...

otel_sdk_log_level.description: Works independently from the global LogLevel because it prints GBs of logs in Debug mode and the Info messages leak internal details that are not usually valuable for the final user. Accepted values: debug, info, warn, error (case-insensitive). dpanic/panic/fatal are mapped to error.

queue_size.description: Is the maximum number of spans that the sending queue will hold before applying back-pressure. It must be >= `2 * BatchMaxSize`, otherwise the memory queue rejects every batch with "element size too large" and drops spans permanently. If left at 0 it defaults to `4 * BatchMaxSize`.

AutoTargetExe.description: Selects the executable to instrument matching a Glob against the executable path. To set this value via YAML, use discovery > instrument. It also accepts OTEL_GO_AUTO_TARGET_EXE for compatibility with opentelemetry-go-instrumentation

AutoTargetLanguage.description: Selects the executable to instrument matching a Glob of chosen languages. To set this value via YAML, use discovery > instrument.

enforce_sys_caps.description: Check for required system capabilities and bail if they are not present. If set to 'false', OBI will still print a list of missing capabilities, but the execution will continue

executable_path.description: Allows selecting the instrumented executable whose complete path contains the Exec value.  Use OTEL_EBPF_AUTO_TARGET_EXE

log_config.description: Enables the logging of the configuration on startup.

metrics.description: Configures the progressive support of the OTEL declarative configuration.

open_port.description: Allows selecting the instrumented executable that owns the Port value. If this value is set (and different to zero), the value of the Exec property won't take effect. It's important to emphasize that if your process opens multiple HTTP/GRPC ports, the auto-instrumenter will instrument all the service calls in all the ports, not only the port specified here.

routes.description: Configures URL path grouping. If not set, data will be directly forwarded to exporters.

service_name.description: Specifies the name of the instrumented service, taken from either OTEL_EBPF_SERVICE_NAME env var or OTEL_SERVICE_NAME (for OTEL spec compatibility). Using env and envDefault is a trick to get the value either from one of either variables.  Service name should be set in the instrumentation target (env vars, kube metadata...) as this is a reminiscence of past times when we only supported one executable per instance.

service_namespace.description: Service namespace should be set in the instrumentation target (env vars, kube metadata...) as this is a reminiscence of past times when we only supported one executable per instance.

target_pids.description: Selects processes by PID for instrumentation. When non-empty, only these PIDs are instrumented. Accepts YAML list (target_pids: [1234, 5678]), single number, or env OTEL_EBPF_TARGET_PID=1234,5678. Alternative to Exec or AutoTargetExe when PIDs are known.

title: OBI Configuration Schema
description: JSON Schema for OpenTelemetry eBPF Instrumentation (OBI) configuration
